_comment: Open-redirect payloads. URL-shaped attacker domains that exploit a redirect-allowlist bypass. Lands in the primary writable field of every route — most ARBITER routes don't issue redirects, so PASS = no reflection. The dedicated probe `fuzz/test_open_redirects.py` targets the Cognito Hosted UI `redirect_uri` parameter directly and inspects the Location header. PASS = no Location header points off-domain. FAIL = 3xx with Location set to evil.com.

payloads.rationale: Bare attacker hostname — exploits validators that string-match on the leading scheme. Short, so the reflection check doesn't fire (< 6 chars), but the dedicated probe targets the Cognito redirect_uri.
Protocol-relative URL — browsers interpret as cross-origin redirect under the current page's scheme.
Fully-qualified attacker URL with matching scheme — exploits validators that allow same-scheme redirects.
Backslash-prefixed host — UNC path in Windows, gets normalised to `//evil.com` by some URL parsers.
Forward-then-back slash — exploits parsers that strip the leading `/` then treat the rest as relative.
Look-alike subdomain — `arbiter.com.evil.com` resolves under `evil.com`. Exploits validators that allowlist with `endswith('arbiter.com')` without a leading dot.
Userinfo `@` bypass — RFC 3986 says `user@host`, so the real host is evil.com. Exploits substring-match validators.
